observations: Lines 5-9: opener stays inside the guide's 7-14 word centerline, then breaks pressure with short fragments ('The lead was measured in ovens now. Shutters opening onto breakfast tables.') instead of flattening into medium-sentence prose.
Lines 37-39: three one-line sensory beats ('Warm yeast. Woodsmoke. Sweetness underneath.') earn their isolation and satisfy the emphasis rule by arriving after fuller road paragraphs.

Lines 81-84: Jaina's tactile read of the grain stretches into a longer introspective sentence, then punctures it with two blunt lines ('Here it still held together. / Here it could still be broken.') exactly as the introspection standard requires.
Lines 167-173: the service-lane survey risks fragment monotony, but the whistle interrupt at line 173 resets cadence before the list can turn metronomic.

Lines 271-303: the gate confrontation maps power through rhythm well—Arthas gets clipped demands ('Well?' / 'How many districts?' / 'Can the city still be saved?'), while Jaina carries the longer evidence-bearing clauses before snapping down to 'No.'
Lines 17-29: Jaina translates mechanism into street-useful language ('service gate,' 'Bakehouses,' 'that block is gone') instead of hiding in abstract magical terminology.
Lines 77-104: magical vocabulary stays embodied and world-native—'pull,' 'preserving shell,' 'weave,' 'frost'—with no lab-report leakage or modern technical jargon.
Lines 146-159: the chapter names the real problem in bread, steam, hands, and district distance rather than summarizing with planner or briefing language.
Lines 221-231: Jaina's counting pass remains concrete—rolls, baskets, kitchens, stomachs—so the conclusion is earned from visible carriers instead of narrator summary.

Global search check: no banned document-voice offenders from the anti-slop hitlist or style guide survived in narration or dialogue (no vector/source-path jargon, no process-language abstractions, no therapeutic or scientific memo diction).
Line 9: 'The lead was measured in ovens now' is a clean pressure image sourced from the chapter's working reality; it sharpens urgency without ornament.
Line 33: 'an ordinary morning that had not yet learned what it was carrying' is mild personification, but it stays tied to dread escalation rather than decorative flourish.
Line 96: 'the way a hand reaches into foul water for the bottom' is a single tactile comparison that clarifies method and bodily revulsion in one stroke.
Line 269: 'the city's oldest oath dragged into armor' uses institutional/court-life imagery appropriate to Uther's moral weight; it does not collide with the chapter's other source domains.

Whole-chapter density check: figurative language stays sparse at roughly 1.6 fresh images per 1000 words, below the style guide's nominal 2-4 band but consistent with the chapter's tightening horror pressure. No mixed metaphors, stacked similes, or epic-fantasy abstraction clutter found.
Paragraph-cadence check: the chapter lives overwhelmingly in 1-4 sentence paragraphs, matching the style guide's default visual rhythm.
Lines 5, 167, 171, 207, 261, and 269 are the densest descriptive/thought paragraphs; each is followed by shorter beats, dialogue, or motion, so the page never stacks dense blocks back to back.
Line 269 runs about 108 words across 7 sentences—dense but still under the guide's ~120-word ceiling, and it is justified by threat assessment plus entrance staging.
Scene boundary at line 144 uses a clean break into the bread-in-hands reversal rather than a summary bridge, so the chapter's midpoint turn lands with proper force.

Line 173 violated the transition convention by leaning on temporal scaffolding ('Then'); revised to an immediate carrier detail ('A whistle cut through the lane.') so the move is driven by event, not by clockword.

Line 261 also leaned on clock scaffolding ('By the time'); revised to 'At the southern approach, Arthas's column hit first as sound, then sight' so the gate approach enters through sensation and consequence instead of summary timing.

fixes.rule: sceneTransitionConventions — avoid temporal scaffolding unless clarity requires it
fixes.before: Then the whistle cut through the lane.
fixes.after: A whistle cut through the lane.

fixes.rule: sceneTransitionConventions — move by consequence rather than clock time
fixes.before: By the time they reached the southern approach, Arthas's column had become sound before sight.
fixes.after: At the southern approach, Arthas's column hit first as sound, then sight.

observations: Overall dialogue-to-narration ratio lands around 17:83. That is far below the style guide's chapter-level 45:55 target, but the distribution tracks scene type: ride/opening roughly 20:80, warehouse-action core roughly 5:95, and the gate confrontation rises to roughly 26:74. I did not force this upward because adding speech would violate the chapter's pressure logic.

Before fixes, creative spoken tags sat at 4 of 27 attributed lines (about 14.8%), above the guide's under-8% preference. After fixes, that drops to 2 of 27 (about 7.4%), which brings the chapter back under the cap without sanding off acoustically justified pressure.

Line 11 keeps 'called' on meta-audit review because the riders are mounted and separated by road noise; that tag contributes real sound texture. Lines 13 and 47 did not earn that extra force and were flattened to no-frills attribution.
Line 191 keeps 'snapped' because the command is an interruption beat under active threat; this is one of the rare places where a stronger tag changes the pressure instead of merely decorating it.

Lines 154-161, 251-257, and 291-303 rely on short untagged exchanges, but speaker clarity holds because the scene has only two live voices at a time and each exchange is pinned by surrounding action or hierarchy.

Action-beat placement matches spec more often than not: post-line beats and hinge beats dominate ('"There." She pointed.' at line 41; '"Maps." She held out her hand.' at line 128; 'Sylvanas stepped closer and read her once. "Worth another?"' at lines 233-233).

fixes.rule: attributionStandards — no-frills tags should dominate routine attribution
fixes.before: "And if the grain's already split?" Cyndia called.
fixes.after: "And if the grain's already split?" Cyndia asked.

fixes.rule: attributionStandards — creativeTagPolicy should stay contextual and sparse
fixes.before: "Because nobody expects to defend a city from its own bread," Jaina muttered.
fixes.after: "Because nobody expects to defend a city from its own bread," Jaina said.

observations: Lines 9-19 and 33-39: Jaina's default attention runs to routes, ovens, carriers, and district order—the exposition filter stays locked on mechanism and consequence rather than social ornament.
Lines 96-104 and 195-207: speculative comfort is very high in the correct Jaina way; magic is narrated as structure, pressure, preserving shell, and failure mode, never as spectacle for its own sake.

Lines 150-159 and 301-309: emotional register stays intellect-first on the surface and body-first underneath—she keeps working the map, the count, and the answer while stress appears through shaking hands, ash on the tongue, and the taste in her mouth.
Lines 171-197 and 227-231: Jaina's thought pattern follows observation -> pattern recognition -> conclusion. She does not leap to abstract thesis; she counts pallets, carts, hands, and mouths first.

Lines 47, 167, and 297-303: humor and honesty stay inside her fingerprint. The one dry line about a city defending itself from bread is brief and bitter; later the wit dies, leaving only blunt truth. Her power posture also tracks spec: under Arthas's pressure she takes a longer explanatory line, then cuts down to the hard answer when evasion becomes waste.
Adverb count check: roughly 14 clear -ly adverbs across about 3,159 words, or about 1.1 per 250-word page—well under the hard cap of 4 per page.
Passive-voice check: rough scan finds about 2.0% passive constructions, comfortably under the 4% ceiling.
Show/tell balance clears the floor. Lines 146-148, 221-231, and 301-309 all let bread, hands, ash, and bodily response carry the conclusion instead of replacing scene work with labels.
Filter-word sweep stayed clean. The chapter does not lean on noticed/saw/heard scaffolding to create POV; most sensation arrives directly through the viewpoint body's contact with the world.

Paragraph emphasis and compression work with the quality floor instead of against it: single-line beats such as 'Too many.' (line 126), 'Pain now.' (line 211), and 'Uther shut his.' (line 307) earn their emphasis through prior setup rather than melodramatic declaration.

Exposition integration passes. Lines 17-29, 77-104, and 285-285 deliver the plague mechanism only when Jaina needs to act, decide, or answer; there are no glossary paragraphs or neutral-narrator asides.

Power-dynamic rendering passes. At lines 271-303 Arthas seizes ground with short questions and declaratives, Jaina is forced into longer proof-bearing lines, and Uther's moral weight enters mostly through stillness and restraint at lines 289 and 307.

Scene shape holds. The chapter opens task-first in live motion, pivots decisively at the bread-in-hands reversal and whistle reveal (lines 146-191), and resolves with a hard consequence at the gate rather than a soft coda.

Speculative integration matches the domestication rule. Lines 94-104 and 195-207 treat magic as labor, contamination logic, and tactical failure mode; there is no awe-language or decorative spectacle.

Subtext and humor both track the guide. Lines 154-161 ('Jaina.' / 'Good.') and 233-241 ('Worth another?' / 'No.') carry relational pressure well below the spoken surface, while humor correctly thins from the early dry bread line at 47 to nothing by the atrocity-adjacent gate answer.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — lines 17-29, 77-104, and 285-303 stay precise, readable, and arcane without slipping into modern scientific or bureaucratic diction.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — longer analytic lines appear when Jaina is reasoning, then collapse into blunt short answers at lines 126, 237, and 303.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — imagery stays sparse and functional; the chapter does not burden Jaina's lane with ornamental comparisons.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — fear and guilt surface through body strain, urgency, and over-focus on method rather than overt self-naming.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — Jaina repeatedly moves from observation to pattern to conclusion before speaking, especially at lines 171-231.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — bread heat, sour magic, texture in the air, and bodily cost of working the weave dominate the sensory field.

extraChecks.humorType: pass — one dry, tired line early; humor thins appropriately as horror firms up.
extraChecks.powerPosture: pass — under pressure she lengthens into evidence, then refuses false hope with short honest lines.
extraChecks.speculativeComfort: pass — magic reads as native craft, pressure, and failure mode.
extraChecks.expositionFilter: pass — she notices carriers, stock, routes, kitchens, wards, and consequence before abstract meaning.

metrics.dialogueToNarrationRatio: 17:83 overall; ride/opening ~20:80, warehouse action ~5:95, gate confrontation ~26:74
